nodes.context: Predominantly Jewish-American street gang that dominated parts of New York City's underworld from late 1890s to early 1910s.
Italian-American and Italian immigrant street gang under Paul Kelly.
Long dominant Irish street gang succeeded by Eastman Gang and Five Points Gang.
Another gang of the era headquartered in Corlear's Hook area; Eastman Gang may have developed from it.
Local gang rivaling Eastman Gang over territory and political work.
Ally of Eastman Gang according to infobox.
Ally of Eastman Gang according to infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Eventually, the gang became involved in rivalries with other local gangs such as the Yakey Yakes and the Five Points Gang, warring over both territory and work as political sluggers for Tammany Hall.
edges.context: Eastman Gang and Five Points Gang were rivals warring over territory and political work.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Five Points Gang, Whyos, Yakey Yakes
edges.context: Infobox lists Whyos as a rival of Eastman Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: Eventually, the gang became involved in rivalries with other local gangs such as the Yakey Yakes and the Five Points Gang, warring over both territory and work as political sluggers for Tammany Hall.
edges.context: Eastman Gang and Yakey Yakes were rivals warring over territory and political work.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batavia Street Gang, Lenox Avenue Gang
edges.context: Infobox lists Batavia Street Gang as an ally of Eastman Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batavia Street Gang, Lenox Avenue Gang
edges.context: Infobox lists Lenox Avenue Gang as an ally of Eastman Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: Another gang of the era, the Short Tails, had its headquarters in this same area, and the Eastman gang may have developed from that group.
edges.context: Eastman Gang may have developed from Short Tails, indicating a possible predecessor relationship.
